coverageGaps.reason: Required tool(s) not found on PATH: sonar-scanner
Required tool(s) not found on PATH: cover-agent
Required tool(s) not found on PATH: gitleaks

howItWorks: Detect the project ecosystem(s) and select the applicable scanners.
Preflight: verify the app has the inputs needed for a meaningful scan (POC→Enterprise readiness).
Run security/quality/test scanners AND per-component evaluators (fail-safe, robustness, coherence).
Deduplicate findings, compute the readiness score, and evaluate the Quality Gate.
Emit human reports (console / HTML / PDF) and a machine fix-manifest for coding agents (Claude Code).

bands.meaning: Caution — address before the next deploy.

model.formula: 100 − Σ ( weight × log₂(1 + count) ) per severity, clamped to 0–100
model.notes: Severity dominates volume (one CRITICAL outweighs many LOWs) and repeated findings of the same severity have diminishing impact. The score is a health indicator — the pass/fail decision is the Quality Gate, not the score.

thisReport.formula: 100 − Σ ( weight × log₂(1 + count) ) per severity, clamped to 0–100

CRITICAL.meaning: An exploitable flaw or breaking defect that puts production at immediate risk.

HIGH.meaning: A serious security or correctness issue likely to cause harm in production.
HIGH.action: Fix before release; blocks the gate by default.

MEDIUM.meaning: A real weakness that increases risk but is not immediately exploitable.
MEDIUM.action: Plan a fix soon; advisory by default.

LOW.meaning: A minor or cosmetic issue with limited impact.

security.label: Security
security.whatItMeans: A vulnerability an attacker could exploit.
security.whyItMatters: Directly exposes the application or its data to attack.

defect.label: Defect
defect.whatItMeans: The application behaves incorrectly (e.g. a failing test).
defect.whyItMatters: Broken logic reaches users and erodes trust.

robustness.label: Robustness
robustness.whatItMeans: Missing resilience: no timeout, no error handling, no input validation.
robustness.whyItMatters: The app fails unpredictably under load, bad input, or upstream outages.

coherence.label: Coherence
coherence.whatItMeans: Components disagree (e.g. a UI call that an endpoint will reject).
coherence.whyItMatters: Mismatches cause runtime failures that unit tests miss.

fail-safe.label: Fail-safe
fail-safe.whatItMeans: A control lacks a safe default or handled failure path.
fail-safe.whyItMatters: When something goes wrong, the app does the unsafe thing.

best-practice.label: Best practice
best-practice.whatItMeans: Deviation from a hardening or quality convention.
best-practice.whyItMatters: Accumulated drift makes the system harder to secure and maintain.

coverage.label: Coverage gap
coverage.whatItMeans: A check could not run (missing tool or input), so this area is unverified.
coverage.whyItMatters: An unverified area is not the same as a safe one.

glossary.gate: The Quality Gate is the deploy decision. It FAILS when the scan finds any issue at a severity listed in `failOn` (default: CRITICAL, HIGH), or when a scanner errored. Otherwise it PASSES. This is independent of the readiness score.

not-production-safe.title: ⛔ NOT PRODUCTION-SAFE
not-production-safe.meaning: Critical inputs or checks are missing — correctness/security is unverified.
not-production-safe.nextStep: Fix the CRITICAL items to reach Production-Safe.

production-safe.title: 🟡 PRODUCTION-SAFE
production-safe.meaning: The security baseline is met, but enterprise-grade hardening gaps remain.
production-safe.nextStep: Close the HIGHLY ADVISED gaps to reach Enterprise-Grade.

enterprise-grade.title: ✅ ENTERPRISE-GRADE
enterprise-grade.meaning: Required and recommended inputs are all configured and verified.
enterprise-grade.nextStep: Maintain coverage as the app evolves.

findings.title: GHSA-4r6h-8v6p-xvw6
findings.rationale: Package xlsx@0.18.5 is vulnerable: Prototype Pollution in sheetJS

findings.title: GHSA-5pgg-2g8v-p4x9
findings.rationale: Package xlsx@0.18.5 is vulnerable: SheetJS Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)

findings.title: COMP-APICALL-NO-ERROR-HANDLING
findings.rationale: State-changing API call POST ${url}/api/v2/import-scan/ has no error handling — a failed request fails silently or crashes the UI.
findings.suggestedFix: Wrap the call in try/catch (or add .catch) and surface the failure to the user; never assume a mutating request succeeds.
